blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Cobb Spring Arts Festival**
📅 Saturday, April 18 | 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM | Jim R. Miller Park, Marietta
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://eastcobbnews.com/event/cobb-spring-arts-festival)

This is the kind of weekend that makes you remember why you live in East Cobb. The Cobb Spring Arts Festival brings together dozens of local artists, live music, and that perfect spring weather you've been waiting for all winter. While you're wandering through booths of pottery, paintings, and handmade jewelry, your kids can grab face paint and watch street performers. It's community at its best — neighbors you haven't seen since fall, artists you didn't know lived five minutes away, and the kind of easy Saturday that doesn't require a plan. The Master Gardener Plant Sale runs alongside it, so you can knock out your spring garden shopping while you're there. Free parking, free admission, and the kind of day that reminds you why weekends exist.
scoring_notes: Perfect family event for established families and empty nesters. Free admission appeals to all income levels. Annual festival offers community connection that East Cobb residents value.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: 20th Annual ATL Blues Festival**
📅 Saturday, April 11 | 8:00 PM | Cobb Energy Performing Arts Centre
🎟️ $25+ | [Get Tickets](https://www.myguideatlanta.com/events/april-2026)

Twenty years running and still the best blues lineup Atlanta puts together. This isn't some tribute band playing covers — these are artists who live and breathe the blues, many with Grammy nominations and decades of touring under their belts. The festival pulls musicians from across the South and beyond, creating one night where you get everything from Mississippi Delta to Chicago electric blues. It's intimate enough to feel personal but big enough to attract serious talent. At $25 and up, it's a fraction of what you'd pay to see these acts individually in Nashville or Memphis. Plus, it's right here at Cobb Energy, so no driving downtown and hunting for parking. Just good music, craft cocktails during intermission, and the kind of Saturday night that makes you feel like you discovered something special.
scoring_notes: Appeals to sophisticated music tastes of educated, higher-income East Cobb residents. Annual event with 20-year history suggests established quality. Price point accessible for $118K median household income.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Taste of Marietta**
📅 Saturday, April 26 | Details TBA | Marietta Square
🎟️ Details TBA | [Learn More](https://visitmariettaga.com/your-ultimate-guide-to-taste-of-marietta-2026/)

Forty-plus restaurants, live music, chef demos, and kids activities all taking over the Marietta Square for one day. Taste of Marietta turns the historic square into one giant food hall where you can sample everything from BBQ to fine dining without committing to a full meal anywhere. The chef demonstrations alone make this worth the trip — you'll actually learn something while you eat. Kids get their own dedicated area with activities and inflatables, so everyone stays happy. It's close enough to East Cobb that you know the restaurants, but far enough away to feel like a real outing. One day, dozens of options, and you get to eat your way through all of them.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for East Cobb demographics - close proximity, food-focused event appeals to educated, higher-income families. Good mix for both families and empty nesters. Annual event so moderate uniqueness, but 40+ restaurants makes it substantial.

venue: East Cobb area (check eastcobbnews.com for exact location)
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Cobb Spring Arts Festival**
📅 Coming up this week | East Cobb
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://eastcobbnews.com/event/cobb-spring-arts-festival/)

This one is right in your backyard and worth clearing your calendar for. The Cobb Spring Arts Festival brings together handmade goods, original artwork, local food trucks, and live entertainment — the kind of afternoon where you wander in for an hour and end up staying three. It's a great place to find something for Mother's Day or Father's Day that nobody else will have. Grab someone, grab lunch from the food trucks, and take your time.
scoring_notes: Solid fit for East Cobb's educated, higher-income demographic who appreciates local arts and artisan goods. The Mother's Day and Father's Day gifting angle adds practical relevance. Loses points on uniqueness — arts festivals are common — but it's local and convenient. NOTE: Exact dates not confirmed from the candidate listing; the East Cobb News event page should be verified before publishing.

event_name: East Cobb Park Concert Series — The Dark Star Brothers
venue: East Cobb Park, 3322 Roswell Road, Marietta
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: East Cobb Park Concert Series — The Dark Star Brothers**
📅 Sunday, April 26 | 3:00–5:00 PM | East Cobb Park, 3322 Roswell Road
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://www.mdjonline.com/news/lifestyle/2026-east-cobb-park-concert-series/article_528d4262-3a5c-4c4a-9857-ce2b79891a8f.html)

The 2026 East Cobb Park Concert Series is back, and next Sunday the Dark Star Brothers take the stage. Bring a blanket, grab some food, and claim your spot on the lawn — this is a Sunday afternoon the way Sunday afternoons are supposed to go. No tickets, no registration, no hassle. Overflow parking is available at the Wellstar East Cobb Health Park if the lot fills up. Free doesn't usually mean this good, so take advantage of it.
scoring_notes: Strong audience match — this is a literal East Cobb event for East Cobb residents, happening right in their park. The recurring series format hurts uniqueness, but the Dark Star Brothers are a specific, one-time date within that series. Good fit for the mix of established families and empty nesters; broad enough appeal that age skew doesn't matter much. Free is a genuine bonus here, not a substitute for quality.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Glover Park Concert Series**
📅 Friday, April 25 | Evening | Glover Park, Marietta Square
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://travelcobb.org/event/glover-park-concert-series-5/)

This year marks 40 years of the Glover Park Concert Series on Marietta Square — and the last Friday of April is the kickoff. Local and national acts take the outdoor stage, and the Square fills up with exactly the kind of crowd that makes it worth going. Dinner before, concert after — or both at once. It's a proper Friday night, it costs nothing, and the Square is good at this. Don't sleep on the first one.
scoring_notes: The 40th anniversary angle gives this recurring series a genuine uniqueness hook for the first show of the season. Strong demographic fit — Marietta Square is well-loved by East Cobb's established-adult crowd, and a free Friday night concert pairs naturally with dinner out. Empty nesters and adults without young kids will find this more appealing than a daytime family event. NOTE: Exact performer and time for April 25 should be confirmed before publishing.

event_name: East Cobb Park Concert Series — The Dark Star Brothers
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: East Cobb Park Concert Series — The Dark Star Brothers**
📅 Sunday, April 26 | 3:00–5:00 PM | East Cobb Park
🎟️ Free — no registration required | [More Info](https://www.newsbreak.com/marietta-daily-journal-561232/4572806211724-2026-east-cobb-park-concert-series)

Sunday afternoon. Blanket on the grass. Live music. Nothing on the calendar that has to happen. That's the whole pitch, and it's a good one.

The Dark Star Brothers play East Cobb Park this Sunday as part of the 2026 concert series — free, no tickets, just show up. Bring a chair or a blanket, pack some food, and stay as long as you want. It's the kind of afternoon that's easy to say yes to and hard to regret.

The concert series runs through May, so if you miss this one, there's another coming. But Sunday's weather window is worth checking — and if it looks good, there's no reason to sit inside.
scoring_notes: Recurring series, which hurts the uniqueness score — but it's the next installment in a series that just started this spring, and each individual concert is a one-time performance. Free outdoor music in a neighborhood park is a perfect fit for an established East Cobb audience that owns homes, values community, and appreciates low-friction weekend activities. Works well for empty nesters and established families with older kids alike. The main trade-off: it's not a can't-miss event on its own, which is why it ranks second.

event_name: Brumby Hall & Garden Historic Tour and Luncheon
venue: Brumby Hall, 472 Powder Springs Street SW, Marietta
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Brumby Hall & Garden Historic Tour and Luncheon**
📅 Friday, May 1 | 11:00 AM – 1:30 PM | Brumby Hall, Marietta
🎟️ Details & tickets at [mdjonline.com](https://www.mdjonline.com/news/events-and-happenings-the-week-of-april-20/article_6cb2f1c2-b457-4945-bddf-e782d3a391f2.html)

Built in 1851 and somehow still standing — Brumby Hall is one of those places most East Cobb residents have driven past without ever stepping inside. That changes May 1.

This Greek Revival landmark has a story worth knowing. It was reportedly spared from Sherman's March to the Sea because of a West Point friendship between the owner and a Union officer. Whether the legend is fully true or partly polished over 175 years, it makes for a great two-hour Friday afternoon. The event includes a guided tour of the house and grounds, followed by a luncheon — so this is a proper outing, not just a quick walk-through.

If your idea of a good Friday involves a beautiful old house, a real story behind it, and a meal with people who appreciate that kind of thing, this one's for you. It books up — don't wait on it.
scoring_notes: Strong demographic fit for the older, educated, homeowner-heavy segment of the East Cobb audience — particularly the empty nesters and established adults who enjoy history, culture, and a proper daytime event. The uniqueness score is high because this is a once-a-year tour of a genuinely significant historic property with a compelling backstory. Audience match scores slightly lower than Taste of East Cobb because it's more niche — not every reader will find a historic house tour compelling — but for the right reader, it's the best event on the list. The Friday timing and luncheon format skews toward retired or flexible-schedule adults.

date: Saturday, April 25 or week of April 25
price: Advance food tickets available; $30 discount card usable at 20+ local restaurants
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Taste of East Cobb 2026**
📅 Coming up this week | East Cobb area restaurants & venues
🎟️ Advance food tickets + $30 discount card available | [Get Tickets & Details](https://eastcobbnews.com/taste-of-east-cobb-announces-2026-restaurants-and-exhibitors/)

This is the one East Cobb food event worth planning your week around. Taste of East Cobb brings together the best local restaurants and eateries in one stretch, letting you eat your way through the neighborhood without committing to just one place for the night. It's a great excuse to try that spot you've been meaning to get to — or go back to a favorite.

The $30 discount card is the move here. You get access to deals at more than 20 local restaurants and eateries, which means it pays for itself fast. Stock up and use it all month. At that price, it's basically free food.

Grab your tickets in advance and bring whoever you've been meaning to make dinner plans with. This one's right in your backyard — no reason to miss it.
scoring_notes: Hyper-local event happening in the newsletter's exact coverage area. East Cobb skews high-income and homeowner-heavy — this audience eats out, spends on food, and cares about supporting local businesses. The $30 discount card is a genuine value play even for a $118K household. Annual event but only happens once a year, and the 2026 restaurant/exhibitor announcement is fresh news this week. Strong fit across all age groups in a mixed established-families-and-empty-nesters audience.

event_name: Springfest at Round Trip Brewing Co.
venue: Round Trip Brewing Co., East Cobb
blurb: Spring finally showed up, and Round Trip Brewing is throwing a party for it. Springfest hits the East Cobb location on May 9 with German-inspired beers, food, craft activities, and live music rolling in as the sun goes down. It's a patio day done right — the kind where you show up for an hour and somehow stay for three. No tickets, no fuss. Just grab someone worth spending a Saturday with and head over.
scoring_notes: Strong audience match for the East Cobb crowd — established adults, empty nesters, and parents of teens who want a low-key, walkable afternoon out. German beer and live music skew adult, which fits well. Free entry with purchase keeps the barrier low even though this audience can afford it. Docked on uniqueness because a brewery springfest is not a rare event, but the hyper-local location (East Cobb's own Round Trip) makes it more relevant than a generic Atlanta pick.

event_name: Kennesaw Concert Series: Boy Band Review
blurb: The Kennesaw Concert Series is back for its third year, and this one's bringing the Boy Band Review. Yes, THAT kind of boy band — the kind that defined middle school dances and road trip playlists for roughly a decade. It's nostalgia, it's outdoors, it's free, and it's close. If you grew up on NSYNC, BSB, or any variation of perfectly synchronized choreography, this is your night. Bring a chair, grab something from a food truck, and let the younger version of yourself have a moment.
scoring_notes: Median age of 42 means this audience was squarely in their prime during the boy band era of the late '90s and early 2000s — the nostalgia angle is a direct hit. Free, outdoor, community concert series fits the established homeowner profile perfectly. It's not a one-night-only headliner, but the specific theme elevates it above a generic 'free concert' pick. Strong demographic and audience match. Deducted slightly on uniqueness because the concert series itself is recurring.

event_name: Sherri Shepherd – Make It Make Sense Tour
blurb: Sherri Shepherd is bringing her stand-up tour to Buckhead Theatre Saturday night and it sounds exactly like what you would expect from her: sharp, funny, unfiltered takes on relationships, pop culture, and everyday life. She has been making people laugh for decades across TV, The View, and now the stage. If you need a night out that does not require a plot summary or a lot of pre-planning, this is it. Grab dinner nearby, walk in, and laugh for two hours. Simple as that.
scoring_notes: Sherri Shepherd has high name recognition with the 40-plus female demographic that makes up a meaningful share of East Cobb newsletter readers. Stand-up comedy at an intimate Buckhead venue is a natural fit for empty nesters and couples looking for a low-friction Saturday night out. Not a one-of-a-kind artist, but the format and familiarity make it an easy yes for this audience.

blurb: If you have never been to the Powder Springs Seafood Festival, this is the weekend to fix that. Three days of fresh seafood, artisan vendors, live entertainment, and outdoor fun at Thurman Springs Park. It is a short drive from East Cobb and the kind of laid-back, spend-the-afternoon setup that works for pretty much everyone. Come Friday evening to beat the crowds, or go Saturday and make a full day of it. Either way, bring your appetite.
scoring_notes: Seafood festivals skew toward adults and established families, which matches this audience well. Powder Springs is close enough to East Cobb to be a natural outing. Price is essentially free to enter with food costs at the reader's discretion. Three-day run gives flexibility, which empty nesters and busy families both appreciate.

blurb: More than 100 local bands, performing on actual neighborhood porches, spread across one of Atlanta's most walkable and beloved neighborhoods for a full Saturday. That is the whole idea behind Virginia Highland Porchfest, and it works every year. You stroll the streets, wander into whatever sounds good, grab food from vendors along the way, and stay as long as you want. There is no stage you have to plant yourself in front of. It is just a great afternoon outside in a neighborhood that knows how to throw a good time. Tickets start at $10.
scoring_notes: Porchfest is a genuinely unique format that the East Cobb audience, educated, culturally engaged, and comfortable in Atlanta, would appreciate. The all-day, come-and-go structure works for both couples and families with teens. Virginia Highland is a natural destination for this demographic on a Saturday. The $10 entry is essentially a non-consideration at this income level. Slight demerit for being Atlanta-centric rather than nearby, but the uniqueness and format make it worth featuring.

blurb: If you have a teenager in the house who plays video games, this one is for them and honestly, you might enjoy it too. DreamHack Atlanta is one of the biggest gaming and esports festivals in the country, and it is happening this weekend at the Georgia World Congress Center. Think Call of Duty and Counter-Strike 2 major tournaments, open play areas, cosplay, an expo floor, and thousands of people who take this stuff seriously. A 3-day family ticket starts at $99 and covers kids under 13 for free. It is a legitimate way to actually do something your kid thinks is cool, which is harder to pull off the older they get.
scoring_notes: East Cobb skews toward families with teen and college-age kids, which is the exact demographic DreamHack targets. The event is massive (global esports competition) and not a typical weekend outing, giving it strong uniqueness. Ticket price is very manageable for a $118K median income household. The family ticket structure makes it easy to bring multiple kids. Strong fit for the 'something to do with teens' gap this audience often has.

venue: Holy Transfiguration Greek Orthodox Church, Marietta
price: Free admission (food and drinks for purchase)
blurb: The Marietta Greek Festival is back for its 36th year, and it is right in your backyard. This is one of those events that has stuck around for four decades for a simple reason: it delivers every single time. Authentic Greek food made from scratch, traditional music, live dancing, and a crowd that actually has fun. It runs Thursday through Saturday, so you have three chances to get there. Pick a night, bring someone who appreciates a good gyro, and let the spanakopita do the rest.
scoring_notes: Duplicate source reference for same event — see candidate_index 7 notes.

venue: Holy Transfiguration Greek Orthodox Church, Marietta
price: Free admission (food and drinks for purchase)
blurb: The Marietta Greek Festival is back for its 36th year, and it is right in your backyard. This is one of those events that has stuck around for four decades for a simple reason: it delivers every single time. Authentic Greek food made from scratch, traditional music, live dancing, and a crowd that actually has fun. It runs Thursday through Saturday, so you have three chances to get there. Pick a night, bring someone who appreciates a good gyro, and let the spanakopita do the rest.
scoring_notes: Annual event but long-running local institution with strong community identity in East Cobb. Greek food, live music, and outdoor festival format hit perfectly for the mix of established families and empty nesters at median age 42. Zero cost to enter keeps it accessible even though the income level makes price a non-issue. Proximity to East Cobb is a major plus. The 36-year legacy gives it credibility and familiarity that resonates with long-time residents.

blurb: Marcus King is the kind of artist you want to see before everyone else catches up. The Grammy-nominated guitarist and singer is reuniting with his full band at the Fox Theatre Saturday night for a show that pulls from his latest album and the fan favorites that built his reputation. His sound sits right at the crossroads of Southern rock, blues, and soul, and he plays it with the kind of conviction that makes a Fox Theatre show feel like something more than just a concert. There are not many artists doing what he does right now, and the Fox is a perfect room for it. Get tickets before the weekend sneaks up on you.
scoring_notes: Marcus King's musical style, Southern rock and soul, aligns well with the tastes of an educated, 42-median-age East Cobb audience. The Fox Theatre is a beloved Atlanta venue that this demographic is comfortable attending and willing to spend on. Grammy-nominated status gives him enough name recognition without being a mainstream pop act. Strong adult date-night energy. The empty nester segment of this audience is especially likely to respond to a Saturday night concert at an iconic venue.
